key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made the detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coating denser and more wear-resistant, though with a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: This shows that tuning a single detonation-spraying parameter can materially improve protective coating performance for high-wear industrial components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings were deposited at barrel filling ratios of 43% and 53%.
Microstructure and phase composition were analyzed using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Tribology was measured by dry ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% filling-ratio coating achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N) and showed better wear resistance under higher load.
watch_item: The improved wear resistance came with increased friction, so application tradeoffs should be assessed for systems sensitive to heat generation or drag.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
